title: Ritsuko Hero Villain or Neither
wikitext:
[]: Ritsuko--Hero, Villain, or Neither?             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Sat Apr 19, 2014 10:09 am Ritsuko Akagi is one of the hardest-to-place characters in the show morally. On the one hand, she ends up (presumably unintentionally) aiding the protagonists by attempting to destroy the NERV supercomputers, thereby halting Third Impact, but on the other hand she was responsible for the destruction of the entire ReiQuarium and apparently has a very condescending attitude toward Rei in general. All that can seemingly be said about her is that, what ever side she is on, she is not on the same side as Gendo at the end of the story. Your thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sat Apr 19, 2014 10:22 am The whole thing about the Reiquarium scene is that she was primarily doing it as a means of getting back at Gendo for turning her over to Seele as well as her other frustrations towards him. Plus, this does not equate to the same as actually killing Rei, only her "soulless" clones that are "empty shells". She's not actually killing a real Rei like her mother did. Plus, her relationship with Gendo might be the only such relationship she's ever had, as the series never explicitly confirms or denies this. When Gendo isn't doing things that get on her nerves, she actually seems like a pretty okay person. True, she's sometimes cold and tactical about mission emergencies, but that's just part of her uber scientist background, being as logical as possible about a given situation without letting emotions get in the way....except if it has to do with Gendo. Ritsuko isn't a villain, not even close. Just the same that Shinji isn't necessarily a villain for jerking off over Asuka's body. All these characters started off from places that were relatively good and were just made worse and worse by the added psychological stresses during the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Sat Apr 19, 2014 11:11 am Ritsuko is just severely misunderstood. I like her character and feel bad how she's often vilified, especially when it comes to teh Rei. Like Shinji, she doesn't recognize her self-worth and settles for Gendo because of self-esteem issues and he validates her existence (somewhat). Hence why she come across as rather unhinged at times. She just needs someone to love, understand, and respect her.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sat Apr 19, 2014 11:15 am ^ In short, she needs Maya!

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Apr 19, 2014 11:33 am Ritsu is really the counterpoint to Misato -- being the dispassionate scientist trying to do the calculated necessary things to save the world, in contrast to the latter's gut-feel improvisational style; then betrayed by her biology when faced with the tallest, brightest, mofo around. So generally neurotic to start with, then simply loses it when her simple jealousy of Gendo's unforced affection for Rei [center] [/center] gets turned into hate when she is handed over to SEELE explicitly as a Rei substitute. So, she ends up on the wrong side in episode 25 for emotional reasons. Oh, and Ritsu/Misato is the approved ship.

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Sat Apr 19, 2014 1:01 pm For me, I like Ritsuko, and sympathize with her, but I'm pretty sure she's a bad person. It's true that during the famous Misato-Ritsuko contretemps you could usually say she's being no worse than outspokenly sentimental. I think this helps conceal, however, that she does this with Maya as well. There's a scene (reproduced in the Manga) where she plays off the same dynamic, but this is into the Descent Arc and it's being played for higher stakes now. Remember it? It's the one where Maya (the 'sensitively vomiting conscience' of Nerv) says she only takes part in the Dummy Plant program against her and Ritsuko's response is...ambiguously terrifying. The last of these dialogues is in ep. 25, where the way she talks suggest to me that she's part of whatever renegade faction of the Seele gospel that Gendo practices. When she rigs TD to blow, you don't know whether she's trying to save the world or she's trying to take everyone with her in a phenomenally grandiose fashion. However, when she (possibly) rigs Jet Alone's disaster, she is at the very least a willing cog in Gendo's ruthless machine. Really, she's a sympathetic person and a brilliant scientist who lets her deeply wounding personal trauma unravel her in the end. I see her the way I see Gendo mostly, but still on this side of the dark shoals that Gendo crossed where now he beckons to you from the far shore. I've always been partial to the 'utopian'/'people are such sad creatures' reading of the Third Impact. Since deciding whether she's done the right thing means working out how EVA-verse works, and who's side she's one means working out how the PLOT works...

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Apr 19, 2014 1:13 pm Related threads http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/15697/Everybody-Hates-Ritsuko/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/12183/Are-Naoko-and-Ritsuko-antagonists/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/6764/Ritsuko-knows-too-damned-much-split/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/5303/Ritsukos-level-of-secret-knowledge/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/635/Truth-about-Ritsuko-rampage-revealed/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/3759/What-REALLY-motivated-Ritsuko-to-turn-against-Gendo-in-23/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/6035/Ritsuko-and-Seele-in-Episode-23/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/10193/So-that-Ritsuko-Seele-scene/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/10213/Ritsukos-emotional-state-after-the-SEELE-incident/

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Sat Apr 19, 2014 4:17 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Oh, and Ritsu/Misato is the approved ship.

[]:             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Sat Apr 19, 2014 4:58 pm I had no idea there was already so much discussion about this. . .

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Apr 19, 2014 6:23 pm She's a deeply misunderstood and controversial character. Most people don't realize she's mostly a victim, first of her mother and then of Gendo. Like most of the cast, her behavior stems from a deep lack of self worth and loneliness. If you disagree I'll hex your livestock.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sat Apr 19, 2014 7:09 pm ^ Not that! How will we survive the winter?

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Sat Apr 19, 2014 8:21 pm Guy Nacks wrote: The whole thing about the Reiquarium scene is that she was primarily doing it as a means of getting back at Gendo for turning her over to Seele as well as her other frustrations towards him. Plus, this does not equate to the same as actually killing Rei, only her "soulless" clones that are "empty shells". She's not actually killing a real Rei like her mother did. Plus, her relationship with Gendo might be the only such relationship she's ever had, as the series never explicitly confirms or denies this.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Sat Apr 19, 2014 8:25 pm Because she knew (or assumed) that Shinji was somehow instrumental in Gendo's plans. She wanted to show him the 'truth' of Rei in order to turn Shinji against his father.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sat Apr 19, 2014 8:42 pm Gob Hobblin wrote: Because she knew (or assumed) that Shinji was somehow instrumental in Gendo's plans. She wanted to show him the 'truth' of Rei in order to turn Shinji against his father.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sat Apr 19, 2014 8:45 pm Mr. Tines wrote: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/5303/Ritsukos-level-of-secret-knowledge/

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Apr 19, 2014 9:45 pm Knowledge is not always power. The more Ritsuko knows the more vulnerable she is if it looks like she might stir up trouble.

[]:             Quote Post by Wanderlust » Sun Apr 20, 2014 3:35 am cyharding wrote: It's been a good while since I have seen that episode, so I could be wrong. But could it be that she wanted to break Shinji (and she knew he was half-cracked at that point) in order to get back at Gendo? Why else would she invite him over, and originally alone (Misato invited herself if I remember right)?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sun Apr 20, 2014 4:19 am Not necessarily evil, but definitely amoral. Her conversation with Maya in ep 14 is very telling.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Sun Apr 20, 2014 4:26 am Kendrix wrote: Not necessarily evil, but definitely amoral. Her conversation with Maya in ep 14 is very telling.

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Sun Apr 20, 2014 11:35 am Monk Ed wrote: This one?
